title: Rooftop Gardens: A Drop-in Learning-Rate Schedule Across Plots
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; every plot's data, labels, and the training/scoring code stay in the evaluator process
strategies: flat schedule m_t=1 -> keeps the hot base LR constant, reproduces the evaluator's flat baseline (~0.1 on every plot): over-shoots the noisy plots and never anneals

flat lower multiplier m_t=0.5 -> the obvious 'just turn the LR down' fix; stops the worst over-shoot and beats the baseline, but the wrong SHAPE (too slow to converge the hard spiral plots, no late annealing) so the geometric mean stays modest

warm-up + cosine anneal -> runs near the hot base LR early so the hard nonlinear plots converge in budget, then anneals to a small floor so the noisy near-linear plots settle; generalizes across every plot and dominates any constant multiplier
a custom-tuned schedule (peak height, warm-up length, anneal floor, or a cyclic/step shape) squeezing the remaining per-plot headroom the cosine leaves
